subject: Product Management
title: Brand Men at P&G
description: A quick, practical guide to Brand Men at P&G.

hook.text: After McElroy's memo, Procter & Gamble created the first product management system in history.

buildup.text: Each brand — Ivory, Tide, Camay — got its own brand man who competed internally for company resources.

discovery.text: Brand men ran their products like small businesses: tracking sales, managing budgets, planning campaigns.

twist.text: The internal competition worked so well that P&G brands started outperforming each other — and everyone else.

climax.text: P&G's model became the template. Companies worldwide copied the brand management structure.

punchline.text: Soap companies taught Silicon Valley how to manage products.

quiz.question: How did P&G's brand management system work?
options: Each brand had its own dedicated manager who ran it like a small business
One manager oversaw all brands equally
Brands were managed by committee vote
